headline: A WhatsApp-borne malware campaign and an unpatchable iPhone boot exploit defined the day while defenders raced to govern AI agents spreading through enterprises.

emerging_trends.text: Defenders are scrambling to instrument and lock down AI agents as attackers learn to hijack them through forgotten legacy systems, a gap widening as roughly 71% of organizations push agents into production.
Crews like ShinyHunters keep proving that stolen credentials and abused integrations now drive the costliest breaches, with no malware involved.
emerging_trends.topic_title: ShinyHunters Breaches Skip Malware and Zero-Days

emerging_trends.text: Apple's silicon-level SecureROM flaw shows how some defects live in hardware where no patch can ever reach.
emerging_trends.topic_title: usbliter8 PoC Reaches Millions of iPhones

topics.title: Legacy Infrastructure Hijacks AI Agents
topics.latest_developments: After a Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit talk, The Hacker News warned that attackers route through legacy infrastructure to hijack the AI agents that 71% of organizations now pilot, while Asymptote Labs shipped Agent Beacon, an open-source telemetry layer that logs what agents such as Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Cursor do across laptops, CI jobs, and cloud environments.
topics.summary: AI agents edit files, run commands, and call outside tools with little oversight, and aging systems hand intruders a foothold to commandeer them. Teams should instrument every agent and treat each as a governed identity.

sources.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
Agent Beacon: Open-source telemetry layer for AI agents

topics.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Drops RMM Software
topics.latest_developments: Kaspersky's Securelist documented a worldwide campaign that distributes VBScript files through WhatsApp and installs a UEMS remote monitoring and management agent through a multi-stage infection chain.
topics.summary: The operation tricks recipients into running VBS scripts that fetch legitimate RMM software, handing operators full remote control of infected machines. Users should refuse scripts that arrive over messaging apps.

sources.title: A VBScript campaign distributed through WhatsApp deploying RMM software

topics.title: ShinyHunters Breaches Skip Malware and Zero-Days
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek analyzed the recent ShinyHunters breaches and found the group inflicts mass damage without malware or zero-day exploits, leaning on stolen credentials and abused integrations instead.
topics.summary: ShinyHunters extorts companies by socially engineering access and looting connected SaaS platforms, the same pattern behind its Kodak breach. Defenders should tighten identity controls and audit third-party integrations.

sources.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks

topics.title: usbliter8 PoC Reaches Millions of iPhones
topics.latest_developments: Researchers published a working proof-of-concept for usbliter8, and SecurityWeek reported the flaw reaches millions of iPhones built on Apple's A12 and A13 chips.
topics.summary: usbliter8 runs arbitrary code inside SecureROM, the boot code Apple burns into its A12 and A13 silicon at manufacture, so no software update can close it; the affected chips power a generation of older iPhones.

sources.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones

topics.title: Debate Sharpens Over Gating Cyber-Capable AI Models
topics.latest_developments: Jaya Baloo, chief operating officer and chief information security officer at Aisle, told Help Net Security that gating cyber-capable AI models misreads how attackers and defenders operate and that open-weight models cut both ways, sharpening the fight over Washington's ban on exporting Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
topics.summary: Policymakers weigh restricting powerful hacking-capable AI models, yet defenders rely on the same tools to guard networks. Baloo argues that access limits widen the gap between attackers and the teams chasing them.

sources.title: Who pays when you gate cyber-capable AI models?

weather: Today: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely, high 78F.
Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms then Mostly Cloudy, low 59F.

games.recap: Gonzalez and Reynolds homer as the Pirates hold off the Rockies 8-6

business_politics.title: U.S.-Iran Talks Press Toward a Permanent Deal
business_politics.latest_developments: U.S. and Iranian negotiators wrapped a second day of talks in Obbürgen, Switzerland, with Vice President JD Vance calling the session a 'good foundation' and mediators floating a plan to ease Lebanon and Hormuz tensions, sending oil lower.
business_politics.summary: Diplomats are working in Switzerland to turn the U.S.-Iran ceasefire into a permanent settlement that governs Strait of Hormuz shipping; more than 400 tankers wait near the strait for a full reopening, and operators refuse to move until the truce firms up.

sources.title: Iran Mediators Offer Plan to Ease Lebanon, Hormuz Tensions
Over 400 ships are waiting near Hormuz for the strait to reopen fully
Stock Market Today: Oil Slips After Tense U.S.-Iran Talks

business.title: Liberty Pole Spirits Makes Bleier Family Whiskey
business.latest_developments: Liberty Pole Spirits has teamed with former Steelers running back Rocky Bleier to produce a Bleier Family whiskey and a rye-bourbon blend it calls Penntucky, the Post-Gazette reported.
business.summary: Liberty Pole Spirits is bottling a branded Rocky Bleier family whiskey and a rye-bourbon blend named Penntucky, joining Pittsburgh craft distillers Wigle and Iron City in the region's spirits trade.

sources.title: Rye + bourbon = Penntucky, and guess who's making Bleier Family whiskey?

around_town.title: Two Synagogues Merge Into Beit Kulanu
around_town.latest_developments: Two Pittsburgh synagogues have combined into a single congregation named Beit Kulanu, the Post-Gazette reported.
around_town.summary: Beit Kulanu—Hebrew for 'a house for all of us'—unites two Pittsburgh Jewish congregations into one body, a consolidation that tracks shifting membership across the region's synagogues.

sources.title: A house for all of us: 2 Pittsburgh synagogues merge to form unified congregation, Beit Kulanu

around_town.title: Murrysville Fights Acid-Mine Drainage
around_town.latest_developments: Murrysville residents are attacking acid-mine drainage fouling streams that feed Turtle Creek, TribLive reported.
around_town.summary: In Murrysville, a Westmoreland County town that markets its wooded streams, residents are pursuing multiple fixes for acid-mine drainage that carries pollution down through stormwater channels to Turtle Creek.

sources.title: Murrysville residents tackling acid-mine drainage problem from multiple angles

around_town.title: Pennsylvania Bills Would Shield Sensitive Spaces From ICE
around_town.latest_developments: State Senator Lindsey Williams and other Pennsylvania legislators introduced a package of bills to bar immigration arrests at schools, hospitals, and polling places, TribLive reported.
around_town.summary: After Congress directed an additional $70 billion to Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol, Williams and her colleagues want to protect 'sensitive' Pennsylvania spaces—schools, hospitals, and polling places—from immigration-based arrests.

sources.title: State legislators propose bills to keep ICE officers away from 'sensitive' spaces

around_teams.title: Pirates' Questions on Cruz, Griffin, and Jones
around_teams.latest_developments: Returning home from a mediocre road trip, the Pirates face fresh questions about O'Neil Cruz, prospect Konnor Griffin, and the health of Jared Jones, the Post-Gazette wrote.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette laid out three issues confronting the Pirates as they open a homestand: O'Neil Cruz's form, top prospect Konnor Griffin's trajectory, and starter Jared Jones, who took a line drive off his surgically repaired pitching elbow.

sources.title: 3 important questions the Pirates face as they return home after a mediocre road trip

team_usa.title: New York Studies Dual-City Winter Olympics Bid
team_usa.latest_developments: New York State announced a committee to study whether New York City and Lake Placid should jointly bid for a future Winter Olympics, ESPN reported.
team_usa.summary: New York is exploring a dual-host Winter Olympics bid pairing New York City with Lake Placid, which staged the Games in 1932 and 1980; the committee will weigh the unusual two-city format.

sources.title: NYC, Lake Placid consider joint Olympics bid

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues that AI companies undercut their credibility by publishing alarming warnings about their own products' dangers, likening the tactic to Ford fretting publicly that its F-150 is too powerful to sell.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Apple Price Increases, Apple Intelligence and the E.U.
reading.summary: Ben Thompson examines Apple finally raising prices while declining to ship its Apple Intelligence Siri features to the European Union, reading both moves through the lens of E.U. regulation.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Building on his scoop that OpenAI spent $34 billion to make $13.07 billion in revenue, Zitron continues his case that the AI investment era is a bubble nearing its end.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
